item.title: [World news | The Guardian] Mike Ashley’s Frasers follows Hugo Boss bid with offer for Australia’s Accent
item.description:
UK billionaire’s fashion group offers £166m for takeover of 77.1% of shares in shoe firm it does not already own

  • Business live – latest updates

The retail billionaire Mike Ashley has launched his second takeover bid in a week, attempting to snap up Australian footwear business Accent Group, days after announcing a tilt at Hugo Boss.

Ashley’s Frasers Group, which already owns the biggest single stake in Accent at 22.9%, said it will offer 65 Australian cents (34p) a share for the remainder of the business, at the same level as its closing price on Friday.

Continue reading...

item.title: [The Kyiv Independent - News from Ukraine, Eastern Europe] General Staff: Russia has lost 1,384,190 troops in Ukraine since Feb. 24, 2022
item.description: The number includes 1,320 casualties that Russian forces suffered over the past day.

item.title: [Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera] What still needs to be negotiated in US-Iran ‘peace deal’?
item.description: The US and Iran say they have reached a deal. Al Jazeera’s Osama Bin Javaid explains how both sides are claiming victory

item.title: [Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera] Trump hails MOU with Iran despite misgivings from some Republicans
item.description: Trump hails MOU with Iran despite misgivings from some Republicans.

item.title: [World news | The Guardian] Andrew Hastie compares AI to cold-war nuclear arms race and warns Australia may fall behind
item.description:
Liberal MP says Australia risks sovereignty and strategic independence being ‘constrained by the AI superpowers reshaping the global order’

  • Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast

Liberal MP Andrew Hastie says Australia should dramatically scale up investment in artificial intelligence to preserve strategic independence and warns the country risks being “a supplicant state” tethered to the US in an era of possible hot conflict with China.

In a major address to Liberal members in Sydney on Monday night, the shadow minister for industry and sovereign capability likened the development of AI to the nuclear arms race of the cold-war era and proposed Australia position itself as a technology hub in the southern hemisphere.

Continue reading...

item.title: [World news | The Guardian] Weather tracker: Saharan heat to send temperatures soaring across Europe
item.description:
Heatwave conditions build over much of continent, while mild start to winter continues in parts of Australia

Hot weather is expected across Europe this week as heatwave conditions build over large swathes of the continent.

A mass of hot air from the Sahara has settled over the Iberian peninsula and spread into southern and western France, pushing temperatures widely into the low- and mid-30s celsius.

Continue reading...

item.title: [Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera] Stepson of Norway’s crown prince jailed for four years in rape case
item.description: Marius Borg Hoiby, 29, convicted of rape, domestic violence and drug charges after seven-week trial in Oslo.

item.title: [World news | The Guardian] Rinehart buys ‘significant’ stake in Elon Musk’s SpaceX – as it happened
item.description:
This blog is now closed

  • Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast

The prime minister and foreign minister have issued a lengthier statement welcoming the agreement made by the US and Iran, and called for continued restraint to avoid further escalation.

President Donald Trump made the announcement he had signed a deal this morning, saying the strait of Hormuz would be reopened.

Continued restraint and constructive engagement will be essential to prevent further escalation and secure a lasting agreement. We are pleased the agreement between the US and Iran includes steps to reopening the Strait of Hormuz …

We encourage all parties to use this opportunity to pursue a durable and lasting peace through dialogue and diplomacy. Iran must address longstanding concerns about its nuclear program and the threat it poses to international security.

Continue reading...

item.title: [Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera] G7 meeting in France: What’s on agenda, who is attending?
item.description: The war in Iran, Ukraine and economic challenges are expected to be a key part of the agenda.

item.title: [Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera] El-Geneina’s struggle: Life amid Sudan war and humanitarian challenges
item.description: El-Geneina residents face soaring food and water costs as aid struggles to meet the needs of displaced families.

item.title: [Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera] How Trump is relaunching a tariff war citing ‘forced labour’ concerns
item.description: Trade analysts predict Trump’s latest push could accelerate the 'reorientation' of global trade away from the US.

item.title: [BBC News] Trump and thousands of others watch UFC fight on White House lawn
item.description: Justin Gaethje beat Ilia Topuria to win the lightweight championship in the main event.

item.title: [World news | The Guardian] EU leaders set to meet after deadly strikes on Ukraine, including Unesco-listed religious site in Kyiv – Europe live
item.description:
G7 leaders to meet in Évian-les-Bains in France later today to discuss the most pressing issues, including Ukraine, Gaza, and Iran

  • Kyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across Ukraine

  • Macron frames Évian G7 agenda in hope Trump will stay for whole summit

in Brussels

Sweden has urged European allies to do more to constrain Russia’s shadow fleet, which keeps oil revenues flowing to fund the Kremlin’s war on Ukraine.

“It is crucial that all member states share the responsibility to constrain the ecosystem that supports these vessels.”

“The shadow fleet is funding drones and missiles hitting Ukraine. If we’re serious about bringing the war to an end, acting against the shadow fleet is not optional.”

Continue reading...

item.title: [World news | The Guardian] Kyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across Ukraine
item.description:
Dormition Cathedral at Unesco world heritage site struck along with residential buildings across capital

  • Europe live – latest updates

A massive Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv has badly damaged the Dormition Cathedral in the Pechersk Lavra monastery complex, a Unesco world heritage site and one of Ukraine’s most significant religious and cultural sites.

Nine people were killed across the country, including four in Kyiv, where waves of drones and missiles drove residents to underground shelters and heavy explosions echoed throughout the capital.

Continue reading...

item.title: [Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera] Pakistani police mistakenly open fire on Australian family, killing child
item.description: Fatal shooting of nine-year-old Hania Ahmed by Punjab Police officer causes furore.

item.title: [World news | The Guardian] Indian outrage over US killing of sailors mounts as leaders attend G7 summit
item.description:
Relations at lowest ebb in years after Washington refused to apologise for deaths in strait of Hormuz

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Fury has continued to mount in India over US refusal to apologise for the deaths of Indian sailors killed in strikes in the strait of Hormuz, further straining relations between the two countries as their leaders meet at the G7 summit in France this week.

Last week, three Indian seafarers, who were working on board commercial oil tankers, were killed when the US launched missile strikes on the vessel as it sailed through the strait of Hormuz.

Continue reading...

item.title: [World news | The Guardian] Starmer says he hopes social media ban for under-16s will come into force next spring – UK politics live
item.description:
Prime minister announces ban, saying social media is making children unhappy and unsafe

  • Social media to be banned in UK for under-16s, Starmer announces

  • UK parents: how do you feel about the under-16s social media ban?

Starmer acknowledges some teenagers will get round these restrictons. But that does not make the rules pointless, he says.

Will it mean that no child ever looks at social media again? No.

But look, this might shock you, but it doesn’t shock parents of teenagers; they get around other laws too.

Some technology companies want us to think that social media is unchangeable, part of an almost natural order.

But we have to resist that kind of learned helplessness. We have agency, we can change it, and we will.

Continue reading...

item.title: [BBC News] Norwegian crown princess's son found guilty of two counts of rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the 29-year-old son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette Marit, has been found guilty of two counts of rape and given four years in jail.
